title: Deceased-data jurisdictional rules matrix
context: The deceased-patient consent pattern (consent-002) includes GDPR Recital 27 and HIPAA decedent-rule notes, but a comprehensive matrix of post-mortem data protection rules across jurisdictions would give adopters clearer guidance. This is HIGH risk-tier: any jurisdictional legal claim must be primary-source-cited and bioethics/legal-reviewed before publication. This is unscheduled backlog; the number of jurisdictions to cover should be determined with the bioethics reviewer. Must depend on consent-002 and compliance-001 being merged.
objective: Research and produce a deceased-data jurisdictional rules matrix (`compliance/deceased-data-jurisdictions.md`) covering post-mortem health-data protection rules across key jurisdictions relevant to the adopting communities (at minimum: EU/GDPR + selected member states with post-mortem laws, USA/HIPAA, UK, Canada, Australia). Each row covers: jurisdiction, legal basis for post-mortem health data, applicable duration/limitations, next-of-kin/authorised-representative obligations, and primary-source citation. Framed as 'informational — not legal advice' throughout.
acceptanceCriteria: Matrix covers at minimum: EU/GDPR + ≥3 EU member states with post-mortem data protection laws (e.g., France, Germany, Italy), USA/HIPAA §164.502(f), and ≥2 other jurisdictions (UK, Canada, or Australia).
Each row includes: jurisdiction, legal basis/statute, applicable duration, next-of-kin/authorised-representative obligations, and primary-source citation.
Every claim is primary-source-cited with the specific statute, article, or section and a retrieval date.
Every page carries 'Informational — not legal advice; consult your legal counsel and DPO for your specific jurisdiction.'
Staleness fields set: `lastVerified` and `validUntil` per jurisdiction; high-change jurisdictions noted for shorter review cadence.
Bioethics/legal reviewer sign-off recorded, version-scoped, before publication.
output: A `compliance/deceased-data-jurisdictions.md` jurisdictional matrix with primary-source citations, `lastVerified`/`validUntil` per row, and 'not legal advice' notices, committed via PR with bioethics/legal reviewer sign-off.
